lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly is this sword, anyway?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Well, that largely depends on who you ask and when you ask it. The sword is not unique, but many like it have been referenced in folktales.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Some tell of great and prideful warriors roaming the lands, cutting down all who oppose them in great crashes of lightning.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Others tell of lovers, one bound by oath, the other free as the wind, cruely torn apart by fate.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Others still tell of three great beasts that alone were powerful, but combined could destroy Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are we still talking about the sword?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Amazingly, yes. In all of these tales, the sword is there, potential shining through the metal, thunder rolling in its wake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are any of these tales... true?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure there are elements of the truth buried in there, but folklore being what it is, I'm inclined to not believe any of it.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Aside from the bit where it can summon lightning. It can absolutely do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the name? You called it Voidwaker?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Well, in a lot of these tales, it's only ever reffered to as someone's sword. Like, [player name]'s sword.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: So, I chose a name for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you chose... Voidwaker? Why?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: There are multiple reasons. I suppose you could say the sword is so powerful that it leaves a void in its wake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That makes sense.

sections.title: Using any other item on her

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: No thank you, I'm currently on a diet.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Ah, greetings. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: I'm Madam Sikaro.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Is that it?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Why shouldn't it be? You asked who I was, and I answered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping to learn something about you, like what you were doing here...!

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Well, perhaps next time you'll ask a more direct question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Making a wizard hideout.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A wizard... hideout?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: A wizard hideout! You know how some wizards have their own towers? Think that but underground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why not just get a tower?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Land price. Kingdoms want you to pay an extortionate amount of money to build on 'their' land, it turns out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So instead you ended up in the basement of a tavern.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Yep. Camarst said I could have it as long as I don't hurt any of his customers too badly.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Not that I actually plan to hurt any of his customers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this part that looks like it's from a weapon...

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Ah yes, that does look like a part of a particularly powerful weapon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I do anything with it?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: You? Perhaps not, though if you could bring me all three pieces, I suspect I could put it back together for you.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: There should be a hilt, a blade and a conduit gemstone. Oh, and I'll want 500,000 coins as payment as well. Good hunting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I could find the other parts?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: I suspect the Wilderness would be a good place to start. There are monsters out there that have grown immense over time.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Seeking out the strongest of them is where I'd start, anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have these parts of a weapon, do you think you could assemble it for me?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Hmm... yes, these are the parts of a particularly powerful weapon indeed. I can reassemble it... for a fee, of course. 500,000 coins, no less.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much on me...

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Well, if you come across some more, come find me again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Curious, you come to me with all of the parts and the coin, yet you change your mind at the last second.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: I do rather get the feeling you're toying with me. Very well, I shall do my part in playing with you.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Now, do talk to me again if you'd like to proceed more sensibly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing with the money from combining the Voidwaker?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: I'll spend some of it improving the hideout. The rest on general expenses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of expenses?

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: Living isn't free, you know? A woman my age has expenses. Food, clothing, protection... It all adds up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Madam Sikaro
lines.text: You walked up to me just to say goodbye? Ok, sure, goodbye.
